data.title: Brunswick (NYSE: BC) surpasses Q4 earnings estimates with EPS of $0.58 vs. $0.57, revenue up $178.8 million.
data.description: On Thursday, January 29, 2026, Brunswick (NYSE: BC) released its Q4 earnings at 06:15 AM. The earnings report revealed that the company exceeded estimated earnings by 2.0%, with an EPS of $0.58 compared to the projected $0.57. Furthermore, revenue experienced a significant increase of $178.80 million from the previous year. In a historical context, Brunswick outperformed EPS expectations by $0.12 in the period reported. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Southside Bancshares Q4 earnings disappoint: EPS of $0.7 missed estimate by -11%, revenue down $3.16M.
data.description: Southside Bancshares (NYSE: SBSI) released its Q4 earnings on Thursday, January 29, 2026, at 05:45 AM. The company reported an EPS of $0.7, missing estimated earnings by -11.0% (estimated $0.79). Revenue decreased by $3.16 million compared to the same period last year. In the previous quarter, Southside Bancshares exceeded EPS expectations. For further details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Celebrity River Cruises adds 10 ships, now offering 20 ships by 2031. 80% more European destinations for 2028 cruises.
data.description: Celebrity River Cruises has unveiled plans to increase its fleet size to 20 ships by 2031, highlighting the opening of bookings for 2028 European sailings. In a recent press release dated January 29, 2026, the company based in Miami disclosed their decision to acquire 10 additional ships to enhance their European river cruise offerings. This strategic expansion will nearly double the current fleet capacity, providing more opportunities for customers to experience river cruises in Europe.

data.title: Doppler raises $9M led by Pantera Capital as default launch infrastructure for onchain assets. $1.5B value created in 9 months.
data.description: Doppler, the primary launch protocol for new DEX pools on Base, has facilitated the creation of over $1.5 billion in value and generated trading volumes exceeding $1 billion within a span of just nine months. This announcement was made in New York on January 29, 2026.

data.title: Reperio Health & Amazon One Medical partner for at-home health screening with instant results, linking to One Medical membership.
data.description: In Portland, Oregon, a partnership between Reperio Health and Amazon One Medical has been established to combine at-home health screenings with proactive healthcare. Individuals who undergo Reperio's unique preventive screening, providing instant results, will now be granted a One Medical membership. This collaboration aims to offer a seamless transition from early detection to continuous primary medical care. This partnership comes at a crucial time as numerous chronic conditions continue to impact individuals.

data.title: Brunswick Corporation announces Q4 and full-year 2025 earnings; detailed release on website.
data.description: Brunswick Corporation (NYSE: BC) announced its financial results for the fourth quarter and full-year of 2025 in Mettawa, Illinois on January 29, 2026. The company's detailed financial results press release can be accessed on its official website at https://ir.brunswick.com. Additionally, the results will be published on the SEC's website through the Form 8-K filing.

data.title: Earnings to be reported on January 29, 2026: Key estimates - Trane Tech $2.82/share, $5.08B rev; Blackstone $1.53/share, $3.66B rev; Honeywell Intl $2.53/share, $9.97B rev; Thermo Fisher Scientific $6.44/share, $11.91B rev; Llyods Banking Group $0.10/share, $6.52B rev. For more details, refer to Benzinga.com.
data.description: Several companies are expected to report their quarterly earnings before the bell, including Trane Technologies with an estimated earnings of $2.82/share on revenue of $5.08 billion, Norfolk Southern with estimated earnings of $2.77/share on revenue of $3.00 billion, and Blackstone with estimated earnings of $1.53/share on revenue of $3.66 billion. Other companies include Tractor Supply (estimated earnings of $0.46/share on revenue of $4.00 billion), Honeywell Intl (estimated earnings of $2.53/share on revenue of $9.97 billion), and Dow (estimated loss of $0.49/share on revenue of $9.48 billion). Companies like Mastercard, Lockheed Martin, and Caterpillar are also expected to report their earnings, with Mastercard likely reporting $4.22/share on $8.76 billion revenue, Lockheed Martin reporting $5.94/share on $19.85 billion revenue, and Caterpillar reporting $4.70/share on $17.84 billion revenue. Other companies in the list include Altria Group, RealReal, and A.O. Smith.

data.title: Royal Caribbean Group to build new Discovery Class ships in France at Chantiers de l'Atlantique shipyard. Jan. 29, 2026.
data.description: In a recent announcement, Royal Caribbean Group (NYSE: RCL) revealed a set of contracts with the Chantiers de l'Atlantique shipyard in Saint Nazaire, France on Jan. 29, 2026. These agreements serve to ensure the building of the upcoming Discovery Class vessels.

data.title: FDA qualifies MolecuLightDX wound measurement tool as a Medical Device Development Tool for wound care products evaluation.
data.description: On January 29, 2026, in Pittsburgh, a company known as MolecuLight revealed that the MolecuLightDX® wound measurement tool has received qualification from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) as a Medical Device Development Tool (MDDT). This program by the FDA is designed to identify validated medical tools, with this initiative aiming to enhance the quality and accuracy of medical devices.

data.title: Solana validators decrease by 68% due to high costs, forcing smaller operators offline in 2023.
data.description: Since 2023, the number of validators on Solana has decreased by 68% due to increasing costs and competition from providers offering zero fees. This has resulted in smaller node operators being forced offline.

data.title: Group 1 Automotive missed Q4 estimated earnings by -9.0% with EPS of $8.49. Revenue up $34.00 million.

data.title: Global sports nutrition market to reach $108.1 billion by 2035 with a CAGR of 7.55%. Rising fitness trends drive growth.
data.description: On January 29, 2026, in New York, it was reported that the Global Sports Nutrition Market is undergoing significant changes due to a rising global interest in physical fitness and performance-based nutrition. This trend is driven by the growing number of people engaging in sports and fitness activities globally. The market is seeing a surge in demand for sports nutrition products as consumers focus more on leading active lifestyles.
